title: Samples catalog is not an enumerable/described/queryable registry — no tool lists the installable block kinds; discovery is filename-convention only.
description: The package registry/ folder (the samples catalog) is reached only by filename guess: installProject takes config.installed_schemas[] names and does path.join(registryRoot, schemas, name+.schema.json) existsSync (index.ts:323-377). There is no manifest/index and no tool that enumerates or describes what is installable. The installed-side discovery functions (availableSchemas/availableBlocks, project-sdk.ts) readdir the project SUBSTRATE (what is already installed), never the catalog. Per DEC-0014 the harness-confined main LLM has NO fs/read tools, so discovery MUST be a tool over a described registry, not a dir read. The raw material exists — every schema carries title + description (DEC-0023) — so a catalog is derivable but not materialized as a queryable surface. read-config (index.ts:884) is the existing exemplar of a queryable registry behind a tool (it returns the config registries as structured JSON); the samples catalog is the missing analog. Intended outcome: a samples catalog that is enumerable + described + queryable via a discovery tool (list installable kinds with title/description/shape), so onboarding can offer them and any LLM can answer what can I add without reading a directory.

evidence.reference: installProject reaches registry/ by name-by-name existsSync against config.installed_schemas — no enumeration, no manifest, no describe
availableSchemas/availableBlocks readdir the installed substrate (what is installed), not the package samples catalog (what is installable)
read-config is the queryable-registry-via-tool exemplar (config registries as structured JSON) to extend to the samples catalog
DEC-0014 (harness-confined main LLM has no fs tools — discovery must be tool-mediated) + DEC-0023 (schema title/description make a catalog derivable) + DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral)

impact: Onboarding cannot offer samples it cannot enumerate; there is zero discovery of the installable set (a user must know schema names a priori to declare them). The queryable-registry-via-tool model (DEC-0014) is realized only for config, absent for the samples catalog. Substrate that the onboarding-elicitation gap (sibling) depends on.
proposed_resolution: Materialize the samples catalog as an enumerable, described, queryable registry behind a discovery tool (list installable kinds with title/description/shape), the analog of read-config for the catalog. Implementation specifics intentionally NOT prescribed (outcome-only per user directive); the implementer MUST first survey the pi-sdk surface + prior art in other pi extensions for canonical best-practice registry/discovery shapes and adopt the exemplar. Distinct from the onboarding ceremony (which depends on it) and from FGAP-056 (hardcoded block types) though same derive-from-registry theme.
